But what happens is that when we develop Java, we have to implement some features that make our software stable,

chunks.title: Need of Encapsulation
chunks.text: First of all, we have to talk about the object.  Every object has some things that it knows and can do.  So basically we can say that an object knows something and an object does something.  For example, let's say if I am a human, then as a human, I know something and I can do something.  In the same way, this keyboard knows something about it.

chunks.text: So this keyboard knows the color, so it has a black keyboard.  In fact, it is gray with shade.  And there are keys in this keyboard, basically a lot of keys.  So basically these are the features of the keyboard.  But what can we do?

And it can do something with the help of methods.  So if you want to store a value, what can an object know?  We will add it with the help of variables and what can an object do?

chunks.text: We will do that with the help of methods.  So as I am talking about you, of course as a human we know a lot of things.  I know my name, my favourite actor, my address, my bank account number.  So all the things are stored here.  Of course I can do a lot of things other than this.

chunks.text: Private means that they are not directly accessible outside.  So here it is written that to the outside world data should not be exposed directly.  Which means all your data should be private, no one should access it directly from outside.  So when these two will work together, then we will use encapsulation.
